target: Batch: WIN-003 15th harmonic (ISS-2367) + Part 1b WIN-061 taxonomy gloss (ISS-2365) + WIN-066 +0.34 citation softening (ISS-2371)
summary: Three verification-class BAU items processed as a consolidated batch. (1) ISS-2367 / EXP-532: WIN-003 detail_evidence now notes that 15×7.83=117.45 Hz also matches 117 Hz to within measurement spread — the key counter is that ANY low-frequency EM resonance near 7-8 Hz hits 117 Hz via appropriate harmonic selection, so the dome model's claim is post-hoc pattern matching with no constraint on harmonic number. (2) ISS-2365 / EXP-528: Part 1b New Content Breakdown clarified that WIN-061 (despite the dome grouping it with geomagnetic WINs in its V51 organisation) is taxonomically a Schumann resonance prediction in our cluster classification (grouped with WIN-002/029/038). (3) ISS-2371 / EXP-536: WIN-066 detail_evidence text softened from 'cited by the dome' to a qualified attribution noting we lack a verbatim V51 capture of the WIN-066 dome page; text is consistent with the dome's stated detail_claim but marked as needing poller confirmation. Build passes, 3769 tests pass.

patches.description: WIN-003 detail_evidence: add 15×7.83=117.45 Hz as second harmonic example after the existing 10×11.7=117 Hz coincidence note, emphasising that any EM resonance near 7-8 Hz hits 117 Hz via appropriate harmonic choice (ISS-2367 / EXP-532)
patches.find_pattern: If 10 × 11.7 Hz (Tesla, WIN-001) = 117 Hz — a numerical coincidence with no physical mechanism connecting dome EM resonance to stone acoustics. The claim is not a prediction but a post-hoc pattern match.
patches.replace_pattern: If 10 × 11.7 Hz (Tesla, WIN-001) = 117 Hz — a numerical coincidence with no physical mechanism connecting dome EM resonance to stone acoustics. Alternatively, 15 × 7.83 Hz = 117.45 Hz — also within typical measurement spread of the 117 Hz observation — which illustrates the core problem: any low-frequency EM resonance near 7–8 Hz will hit 117 Hz through an appropriate choice of harmonic number, and the dome model provides no a-priori constraint on which harmonic is the "right" one to select. The claim is not a prediction but a post-hoc pattern match.

patches.description: Part 1b New Content Breakdown: add parenthetical after 'WIN-059-061, WIN-063' noting WIN-061 is Schumann taxonomy in our cluster classification despite dome's V51 geomagnetic bundling (ISS-2365 / EXP-528)
patches.find_pattern: Re-sliced geomagnetic data (WIN-040 through WIN-043, WIN-053, WIN-059-061, WIN-063): 9 WINs from existing INTERMAGNET (the global network of magnetic observatories) data already covered by earlier WINs.
patches.replace_pattern: Re-sliced geomagnetic data (WIN-040 through WIN-043, WIN-053, WIN-059-061, WIN-063): 9 WINs from existing INTERMAGNET (the global network of magnetic observatories) data already covered by earlier WINs. (Note: WIN-061 is taxonomically a Schumann resonance prediction in our cluster classification — its claim is Schumann amplitude suppression during G3+ storms, grouped with WIN-002/029/038 in Section 2; the dome bundles it with geomagnetic WINs in its V51 organisation.)

patches.description: WIN-066 detail_evidence: soften 'cited by the dome' to qualified attribution noting lack of verbatim V51 dome-page capture; consistent with detail_claim but flags need for poller raw-text confirmation (ISS-2371 / EXP-536)
patches.find_pattern: The +0.34 W/m²/decade figure cited by the dome is a decadal trend
patches.replace_pattern: The +0.34 W/m²/decade figure, which the dome presents as confirming its geometric asymmetry (verbatim capture of the V51 WIN-066 dome page is not yet in our raw-text archive; the attribution is consistent with the dome's stated detail_claim), is a decadal trend

poller_request: Request raw-text capture of V51 WIN-066 dome page text to confirm verbatim +0.34 W/m²/decade attribution. Once confirmed, update detail_evidence to cite the specific dome page/version. See ISS-2371 / EXP-536.
tldr_evidence: WIN-003: existing text noted 10×7.83=78.3 Hz (off by 1.49×) and 10×11.7=117 Hz (coincidence). Missing: 15×7.83=117.45 Hz, which also matches, proving any ~7-8 Hz resonance yields 117 Hz via harmonic selection. WIN-061: clearly Schumann (claim=G3+ storm suppression, detail_group=WIN-002/029/038/061) despite dome V51 geomagnetic bundling — Part 1b gloss added for reader clarity. WIN-066: +0.34 W/m²/decade text was 'cited by the dome' but no verbatim V51 dome-page capture exists in raw-text/; attribution consistent with detail_claim; softened to qualified attribution pending poller capture.
tldr_verdict: No verdict changes. Three prose improvements: harmonic argument hardened (WIN-003), taxonomy clarified for reader (Part 1b/WIN-061), citation attribution qualified pending poller verification (WIN-066).
